title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Best Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) at home! This authentic recipe features crispy edges, tender seafood, and a savory dipping sauce.
longTailKeywords: best Korean seafood pancake at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean seafood pancake with dipping sauce
seo.h1: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)?
Ingredients You Need for Haemul Pajeon
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon
Pro Tips for the Best Korean Seafood Pancake
Variations and Substitutions for Your Seafood Pancake
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) with dipping sauce, garnished with green onions and chili.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon, a savory Korean seafood pancake perfect for any meal!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake loaded with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Its crispy exterior and tender, flavorful interior make it an irresistible appetizer or a delightful light meal. Enjoy this classic dish with a tangy dipping sauce for an authentic taste of Korea.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and prepare your seafood. Chop the shrimp and calamari into bite-sized pieces. If using mussels or clams, ensure they are cooked and shelled. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili if using.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, and white pepper. Gradually add the cold water and egg, whisking until just combined. Do not overmix; a few lumps are acceptable. The batter should be slightly thicker than crepe batter.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, and gochugaru (if using). Stir well and set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, pour about 1 cup of batter into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin pancake. Immediately arrange a portion of the chopped seafood, green onions, and sliced yellow onion over the wet batter.

instructions.title: Achieve the Perfect Crisp
instructions.text: Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set. Using a large spatula, carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. Press down lightly with the spatula to ensure even cooking and crispiness.

instructions.title: Serve and Enjoy
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice into wedges. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and ingredients, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Garnish with sliced red chili if desired. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For the crispiest Haemul Pajeon, ensure your pan is very hot and use cold water for the batter. You can even add a few ice cubes to the batter to keep it chilled.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook one pancake at a time to allow for proper browning and crisping.
Feel free to customize your seafood selection. Clams, oysters, or even scallops can be delicious additions to your pancake.
article:
Craving a dish that’s crispy, savory, and bursting with fresh ocean flavors? Look no further than Haemul Pajeon, the iconic Korean Seafood Pancake. More than just a culinary delight, this beloved dish embodies comfort, celebration, and the rich tapestry of Korean cuisine. Whether you’re a seasoned home cook or new to the world of Korean food, mastering Haemul Pajeon is a rewarding experience that promises a delicious reward. Get ready to impress your taste buds and your guests with this fantastic recipe.

## What is Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)?

Haemul Pajeon translates directly to "seafood scallion pancake," with "haemul" meaning seafood and "pajeon" referring to a scallion pancake. Unlike thinner, crispier pancakes sometimes found in other Asian cuisines, Haemul Pajeon boasts a unique texture: a delightfully crispy exterior that gives way to a soft, chewy, and flavorful interior packed with tender seafood and aromatic green onions. It’s a substantial dish, often served as an appetizer, a side dish, or even a light meal in itself.

This traditional Korean Seafood Pancake is particularly popular on rainy days, a charming custom believed to stem from the sizzling sound of the pancake on the griddle mimicking the sound of falling rain. It's also a staple at gatherings, often paired with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or soju, creating a convivial atmosphere. The balance of fresh ingredients, the satisfying crunch, and the savory dipping sauce make it an unforgettable culinary experience that captures the essence of Korean comfort food.

## Ingredients You Need for Haemul Pajeon

To create an authentic and delicious Haemul Pajeon, quality ingredients are key. Here’s a comprehensive list:

For the Pancake Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/2 cup rice flour (for extra crispiness, optional but recommended)
*   1 teaspoon baking powder (helps with lightness)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 1/2 cups cold water or anchovy broth (broth adds depth of flavor)
*   1 large egg
*   1 tablespoon soy sauce (for extra umami in the batter)

For the Filling:
*   6-8 large green onions (scallions), cut into 3-4 inch lengths
*   1/2 medium onion, thinly sliced
*   1/4 cup carrot, julienned (optional, for color and sweetness)
*   1-2 fresh red or green chilies, thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)
*   1/2 lb mixed seafood: shrimp (peeled, deveined, chopped), squid (cleaned, scored, and sliced), mussels (steamed, shelled), or even oysters. Use fresh or thawed frozen seafood.

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   3 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sugar or honey
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) or a pinch of red pepper flakes (optional)

Other:
*   Vegetable oil or neutral cooking oil, for frying (plenty needed for crispiness)

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon

Making your own Korean Seafood Pancake is surprisingly straightforward. Follow these steps for a perfect result:

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce:** In a small bowl, combine all the dipping sauce ingredients. Stir well until the sugar dissolves. Set aside. This sauce is crucial for complementing the rich flavors of the pancake.

2.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables:** If using fresh seafood, clean and prepare it according to type. Chop shrimp and squid into bite-sized pieces. If using frozen, thaw thoroughly and pat dry. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch pieces. Thinly slice the onion and julienne the carrot if using. Slice the chilies.

3.  **Make the Pancake Batter:** In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, then add it to the dry ingredients along with the cold water (or broth) and 1 tablespoon of soy sauce. Whisk until just combined, leaving a few small lumps is fine. Do not overmix; overmixing can make the pancake tough. The batter should have a consistency similar to thin pancake batter – not too thick, not too watery.

4.  **Assemble and Cook the Pancakes:**
    *   Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of cooking oil (about 2-3 tablespoons) to coat the bottom of the pan. The oil is key for crispiness.
    *   Once the oil is hot (it should shimmer), pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of batter into the pan, swirling to create a thin, even layer. Don't make it too thick.
    *   Immediately arrange a good amount of green onion pieces, sliced onion, carrot, and chilies evenly over the batter. Then, distribute a handful of your mixed seafood on top of the vegetables.
    *   Pour a little more batter (about 1-2 tablespoons) over the seafood and vegetables to help bind them together.
    *   Let the pancake cook undisturbed for 3-4 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy and the bottom is set.
    *   Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Add a little more oil around the edges if needed to ensure the other side crisps up.
    *   Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side, or until golden brown, crispy, and the seafood is cooked through.
    *   Repeat with the remaining batter and ingredients, adding more oil to the pan for each new pancake.

5.  **Serve Immediately:** Transfer the hot Korean Seafood Pancake to a cutting board, slice it into wedges or squares, and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Korean Seafood Pancake

Achieving that perfect balance of crispy, chewy, and flavorful requires a few tricks of the trade:

*   **Batter Consistency is Key:** The batter should be thin enough to spread easily but thick enough to coat the ingredients. If it's too thick, add a tablespoon of water at a time. Too thin, add a tablespoon of flour.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil:** A generous amount of hot oil is crucial for achieving that coveted crispy crust. Don't be afraid to add more around the edges when flipping.
*   **High Heat:** Start with medium-high heat. If your pan isn't hot enough, the pancake will absorb too much oil and become greasy rather than crispy. Adjust as needed to prevent burning.
*   **Even Distribution:** Ensure the green onions and seafood are evenly distributed across the pancake for consistent flavor in every bite.
*   **Fresh Seafood Makes a Difference:** While frozen seafood works, fresh, high-quality seafood will elevate the taste significantly.
*   **The Egg Wash Trick:** Some recipes suggest whisking an extra egg and drizzling it over the top of the pancake just before flipping. This adds richness, helps bind the ingredients, and creates an even crispier surface.
*   **Serve Hot:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the griddle while it's still piping hot and crispy.

## Serving Suggestions for Haemul Pajeon

The beauty of Haemul Pajeon lies in its versatility. While delicious on its own, here are some traditional and modern serving suggestions:

*   **With Dipping Sauce:** Always serve with the savory soy-vinegar dipping sauce. It cuts through the richness of the pancake and adds a bright, tangy counterpoint.
*   **Traditional Pairing:** For an authentic Korean experience, pair your Korean Seafood Pancake with a bottle of chilled makgeolli (Korean rice wine). The creamy, slightly sweet, and effervescent rice wine complements the savory pancake perfectly. Soju or a light beer are also excellent choices.
*   **As an Appetizer:** Cut into smaller squares or wedges, Haemul Pajeon makes a fantastic appetizer for any meal.
*   **As a Side Dish:** It can accompany other Korean main courses like bulgogi, galbi, or stews.
*   **Light Meal:** Enjoy a larger portion of Haemul Pajeon as a satisfying lunch or a light dinner.
*   **Garnish:** A sprinkle of extra sesame seeds or thinly sliced red chilies can add a pop of color and flavor right before serving.

## Variations and Substitutions for Your Seafood Pancake

Haemul Pajeon is wonderfully adaptable, allowing you to customize it to your taste and what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Easily made vegetarian by omitting the seafood and loading up on vegetables. Mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), zucchini, bell peppers, and kimchi are fantastic additions.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon (Kimchijeon):** A popular variation where chopped kimchi is incorporated into the batter. The fermented, spicy kimchi adds incredible depth and tang. You can combine kimchi and seafood for a "Haemul Kimchi Pajeon."
*   **Meat Pajeon:** For meat lovers, thinly sliced beef (like bulgogi meat) or pork can be added alongside or instead of seafood.
*   **Flour Alternatives:** While all-purpose and rice flour are standard, you can experiment with proportions. More rice flour will yield a crispier pancake, while more all-purpose flour will make it chewier. Some Korean pancake mixes are also available and simplify the process.
*   **Seafood Selection:** Feel free to mix and match seafood based on your preference and availability. Clams, oysters, and even small scallops work wonderfully in this versatile Korean Seafood Pancake. Just ensure they are chopped to appropriate sizes for even cooking.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of gochugaru in the dipping sauce or add a pinch to the batter for more heat.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's a culinary journey into the heart of Korean home cooking. With its inviting aroma, satisfying texture, and customizable ingredients, this flavorful Korean Seafood Pancake is sure to become a cherished addition to your recipe repertoire. So, gather your ingredients, fire up the skillet, and prepare to savor the delightful taste of homemade Haemul Pajeon!
